findings.title: Potential SQL Injection (CWE-89)
findings.description:
**Potential SQL Injection Vulnerability**

**Pattern detected**: JavaScript template literal with interpolation in SQL query

**Location**: packages/tailwindcss/src/utilities.ts:1811

**Code snippet**:
```javascript
staticUtility(`select-${value}`, [
```

SQL injection occurs when untrusted input is incorporated into SQL queries without
proper sanitization. An attacker could manipulate the query to:
- Access unauthorized data
- Modify or delete database records
- Execute administrative operations
- In some cases, execute operating system commands

This vulnerability is classified as **CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special
Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')**.
findings.why_it_matters: SQL injection is one of the most dangerous vulnerabilities, allowing attackers to access, modify, or delete sensitive data in the database.

findings.title: Prototype pollution: Object.assign merge
findings.description:
Object merge/extend operation with user-controlled input can pollute Object.prototype.

**Analysis:**
🔍 Pattern: Object.assign merge
⚠️ User input from: `input`
📦 In function: `substituteAtSlot` (3 callers)
findings.why_it_matters:
Prototype pollution allows attackers to inject properties into Object.prototype, which affects ALL objects in the application. This can lead to:
• Remote Code Execution (via gadget chains)
• Denial of Service
• Authentication bypass
• Property injection

findings.title: Mutual recursion: 2 functions in call cycle (complexity 43)
findings.description: Mutual recursion detected: 2 functions form a call cycle. Cycle: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/tailwindcss/crates/ignore/src/dir.rs::impl<Ignore>::matched:365 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/tailwindcss/crates/ignore/src/dir.rs::impl<Ignore>::matched_ignore:408. Aggregate complexity: 43.
findings.suggested_fix: Consider refactoring to eliminate direct mutual calls. Common strategies: merge the two functions, use a callback parameter, or introduce a shared data structure that both functions operate on.
findings.why_it_matters: Mutual recursion creates tight coupling between functions, making them impossible to understand, test, or refactor independently. It can also cause stack overflows if the recursion depth is unbounded.

findings.title: Global mutable variable: compiler
findings.description:
Global mutable state 'compiler' makes code hard to reason about.

**Impact Analysis:**
📊 Used 10 times in this file
⚠️ Module is imported by others - global may leak
findings.suggested_fix:
Cross-module globals are especially dangerous. Consider:
1. Export a getter/setter function instead: `getCompiler()`, `setCompiler()`
2. Encapsulate in a class with controlled access
3. Use dependency injection
findings.why_it_matters: Global state causes hidden dependencies between functions. Changes to globals can have unexpected effects throughout the codebase.

findings.title: Empty catch block swallows exceptions
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] This catch block silently swallows exceptions, hiding potential bugs.

**Risk Assessment:**
⚠️ `readFile` appears to do I/O
findings.suggested_fix:
This swallows I/O or network exceptions - very dangerous!
At minimum, log the exception:
```python
except Exception as e:
logger.error(f"Operation failed: {e}")
```
findings.why_it_matters: Swallowed exceptions hide bugs and make debugging extremely difficult. When something fails silently, you may not know until much later.

findings.title: Blocking call `readFileSync` in async function
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 3 co-located issues] Synchronous blocking call inside async function will block the event loop, preventing other async tasks from running.

**Analysis:**
📦 In async function: `getPackageVersion`
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace with async alternative: `await fs.promises.readFile()`

Example:
```python
# Instead of: time.sleep(1)
await asyncio.sleep(1)

# Instead of: requests.get(url)
async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
async with session.get(url) as response:
data = await response.json()
```
findings.why_it_matters: Blocking calls in async code prevent the event loop from processing other tasks. This defeats the purpose of async/await and can cause the entire application to hang.

findings.title: Regex compiled inside loop

```rust
for item in items {

}
```
findings.why_it_matters: Regex compilation involves parsing and optimization. Doing it once instead of N times can dramatically improve performance.

findings.title: Test code in production: 1 issue

Test utilities and patterns found in what appears to be production code.

**Found:**
• Test assertions (line 30)
findings.suggested_fix:
Options:

1. **Move to test files:** If this is test code, move it to a test directory
2. **Use TYPE_CHECKING:** For type hints only, wrap imports in `if TYPE_CHECKING:`
3. **Environment check:** If needed at runtime, guard with environment variable

```python
# Instead of:
from unittest.mock import Mock

# Use:
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
if TYPE_CHECKING:
from unittest.mock import Mock
```

findings.title: Circular dependency: 2 files
findings.description:
Mutual dependency between: canonicalize-candidates.ts ↔ expand-declaration.ts

**Weakest link to break:** `canonicalize-candidates.ts` → `expand-declaration.ts` (1 import)
findings.suggested_fix:
🔗 **Best place to break:** `canonicalize-candidates.ts` → `expand-declaration.ts` (weakest coupling: 1 imports)

Since `canonicalize-candidates.ts` only imports 1 symbol(s) from `expand-declaration.ts`, consider:
- Move those symbols to a shared module
- Pass them as parameters instead of importing
- Use dependency injection

**For small cycles:**
1. Merge tightly coupled modules if they're always used together
2. Use TYPE_CHECKING (Python) or type-only imports (TS) for type hints
3. Extract common code to a third module
findings.why_it_matters: Circular dependencies make code harder to understand, test, and maintain. They can cause import errors at runtime and make it difficult to refactor individual modules.

findings.description:
== performs type coercion which can cause subtle bugs.

**Analysis:**
📦 In function: `upgradeToFullPluginSupport` (1 callers)
findings.why_it_matters:
Type coercion in == can cause unexpected behavior:
• '1' == 1 is true
• '' == false is true
• [] == false is true
Use === to compare both value AND type.
